transcript.text: I'll do another video request. This is also from George for title.
What's the difference between an apartment and a van? Pros and cons.
He also asked, thanks for the videos, are you smoking weed again?
No, I'm not smoking weed again because it's illegal.
But I will start smoking at the beginning of the year.
I was smoking a little bit last week though.
Okay, somebody else asked me to do this video a little bit ago too.
The difference between living in a van and living in an apartment.
Maybe I'm doing this video a little bit prematurely because I haven't completely moved into the apartment.
Even though I've been here like, what is it, the 14th and I came here on the 23rd.
Almost a month, holy shit.
Like three weeks.
To be honest, I've been kind of depressed since I moved in here.
Because I'm not used to it.
I'm used to moving around and new scenery.
And now I'm kind of like, I'm like here now.
But probably the main reason I'm depressed is I'm a little bit worried about money because I've been spending way too much moving in.
But living in an apartment, man, and you're not moving around, it's kind of like stagnating.
You know, like stagnant water in a pool is not as healthy as river water.
You know, it's going to develop spores and stuff that it'll be less, you know, you don't want to drink it.
You know, fish can't live in it and stuff.
And you're running the risk of having that kind of a life if you live in an apartment because you're just not moving physically.
I think moving around physically helps you for your mind.
You know, like if you drive around town, it just keeps you alive.
If you're staying in one place, it keeps you, it can make you feel a little bit depressed.
Now with me, that was never a problem.
You know, like living in Lafayette, I was just in one place, holed up in the whole place all summer.
Or, you know, living in my car, I'm holed up in like a Starbucks or whatever all the time.
That didn't have an effect on me because I was busy with my timeline.
It was kind of like I was moving in my mind with my imagination, listening to the audio books and stuff.
And if you're moving in your mind, that's enough movement to keep you happy and stuff.
But I think you need to try more.
You need to make more of an effort to do different activities when you live in an apartment.
Then if you're in a car, you don't really have to try.
Like living, people don't understand.
Living in a car is easy.
Like once you get used to it, it's easy.
It's an easy ass life.
That's why, you know, bums living on the street.
Those people have easy lives, man.
You know, if somebody's like, oh, those bums, they have such hard lives.
It's like, how can somebody who doesn't ever, doesn't have to get up to go to work have a hard life?
They have easy lives.
They just hang around.
I mean, sure, they're dirtier.
Is it hard to be dirty?
Not if you're used to it.
But people who have hard lives are like low-end workers, you know, minimum wage workers who have to work nine to five.
God, my heart goes out to those people.
We need to raise the minimum wage.
That's hard.
That's a hard life.
You know, some rich person is like, I worked hard for my money.
You don't work as hard for your money as the local janitor who works 40 hours a week.
Try to take his job for a couple weeks.
You don't kill yourself.
So, but, you know, I mean, yeah, because physical work is hard, you know, harder than sitting around doing, pushing buttons on a computer and talking to people.
That recharges you.
But, but, uh, um, what are we talking about?
I totally spaced it.
What?
Um, oh yeah, difference between an apartment and a van.
Um, pros and cons.
Okay, so, a positive thing about living in an apartment is you get to, uh, you get to have more stuff.
Um, and, uh, you get to, um, you know, do more activities because you can have more stuff.
You know, I can go biking and all that, living in a car, um, you can, um, well, you can cook more food, different kinds of food.
Now, the pros of living in a car is that your life is easy.
You're, you're spending less time on your day maintaining it.
Um, and living in an apartment like me, I, every day I have to spend a few minutes watering my plants.
I gotta clean a lot more.
You know, now I have to vacuum and then I have to shampoo the rug or whatever.
And then I have to reorganize and I have to put my stuff up.
You don't have to do any of that living in a van.
Like living in a van, you don't, you, you can spend all of your time doing something else.
And, uh, that's, that makes it easier.
Um, um, yeah.
I guess living in an apartment you can make, um, you can be more social.
Cause you're not, you know, cause you know people you're, you're stagnant in some town and you can go hang out with your friends and stuff.
You live in a car and you're moving around.
You don't know people.

Um, and you're kind of, and, uh, you just, you get more, um, you get more interested in interacting with people on the street when you're living in a car because you're not, because you don't know people.
If you live in an apartment, um, you're, you're less likely to like be outgoing or at least I am.
Um, because you know, I'm already interacting enough with people at work, my roommate, you know, people I know.
And so, um, um, but overall, um, I would, I think living in a car is better.
Uh, the only reason that I live in an apartment here is because it's Boulder and it's a baddest part of Boulder and it's a baddest apartment.
It's right next to my mom's house and it's cheaper rent than if I wasn't the owner of it.
Uh, I have to pay rent to my brothers though, cause it's their apartment too.
We inherited it from our dad, but, um, um, it's, uh, yeah, it's more expensive.
Um, if it wasn't for this, I think it's better to live in a car for me, for my lifestyle working, um, and on the computer.
All the time.
Um, but just because of the situation that I have, I'm going to have to say that living in an apartment is better, even though I haven't really done it.
I haven't done it for long enough to really be able to talk about it, but, um, I will miss moving around and traveling around.
There's so many towns that I don't know and I haven't seen, but the actual day-to-day life, um, I can, uh, I can, you know, visit.
My mom and stuff.
But to be honest, when I was living in my car, I was not done.
I was perfectly happy, not bored at all.
Um, I was ready to go to the next town, but you know, stuff changed, car breaks down.
Um, and, uh, yeah, it's harder to, um, it's kind of harder to get to sleep living in an apartment because I can do the internet all the time.
You know, I didn't have the greatest, uh, complete, uh, electrical system set up and, and internet in my car system.
Um, and so when I got to bed, you know, the only thing I had to do is listen to the MP3.
And, uh, but here I can surf the internet, which can keep me awake and that's actually can be kind of like a negative thing.
Living in an apartment, a pro, a big pro is you can, I can upload more videos because I can just have that thing uploading videos all night long.
You can't do that living in a car.
So I'm going to do that big time.
Um, and, uh, um, yeah, and you can cook more stuff.
Anyways, that's all I can think about.
Good request.
I should do, I should do this one again in like a year.
Okay.
Peace out.
